title: Color palettes in athanor
code: library(athanor) library(dplyr) library(ggplot2) library(Matrix) library(patchwork) library(Seurat)  # helper to display a named color vector as a swatch strip # could also use colorspace::swatchplot() or scales::show_col() show_palette <- function(colors, title = "") {   if (names(colors) %>% is.null()) {     names(colors) <- seq_along(colors)   }    df <- data.frame(x = seq_along(colors),                    label = factor(names(colors), levels = names(colors)),                    fill = unname(colors))    ggplot(df, aes(x = label, y = 1, fill = I(fill))) +     geom_tile(color = "white", linewidth = 0.3) +     labs(title = title) +     theme_void() +     theme(plot.title = element_text(size = 9, hjust = 0.5),           axis.text.x = element_text(size = 7, angle = 45, hjust = 1)) }

text: plot_* functions athanor accept clrs_specific argument. Pass named_colors entry whose names match values metadata column plotted.

title: Color palettes in athanor
text: value data matching entry clrs_specific, athanor fall back default ggplot2 color scale. add colors custom categories, extend relevant palette:
code: n_cells <- 300 n_genes <- 200  obj <- sim_gex_splatter(num_genes = n_genes, num_cells = n_cells,                         splatter_groups = c(0.5, 0.4, 0.1),                         splatter_method = "groups")  obj <- seurat_pipeline(obj, nfeatures_RNA = 0, perc_mt = 100,                        num_features = n_genes, num_pcs = 15, num_dims = 10,                        k_param = 20, cluster_res = 0.5, verbose = FALSE)  # add BCR metadata obj$isotype <- factor(sample(c("IgM", "IgD", "IgA", "IgG"), n_cells,                               replace = TRUE, prob = c(0.35, 0.15, 0.30, 0.20))) obj$mu_freq <- round(runif(n_cells, 0, 0.25), 3) obj <- bin_mu_freq(obj) plot_dimplot(seurat_obj = obj, data_source = "Simulated",              clrs_specific = named_colors$isotype, meta_col = "isotype",              reduc = "rna.umap", title = "Isotype", legend_label = "Isotype",              plot_label = FALSE) plot_vln_feat(seurat_obj = obj,               clrs_specific = named_colors$mu_freq_bins_fewer,               feature = "mu_freq", meta_col = "mu_freq_bins_fewer",               title = "SHMfrequency", reduc = "rna.umap") my_colors <- c(named_colors$isotype, "NA" = "#cccccc") my_colors #>       IgA       IgD       IgE       IgG       IgM        NA  #> "#377EB8" "#FF7F00" "#E41A1C" "#4DAF4A" "#984EA3" "#cccccc"

title: Color palettes in athanor
text: plot_color_scale() anchors diverging palette zero maps white positive / negative extremes symmetric. particularly useful Seurat DotPlot output, default color mapping can misrepresent zero falls.    palette argument accepts vector colors, defaulting red–blue diverging scheme pals::brewer.rdbu. Use fill_by = "fill" geoms use fill aesthetic rather color.
code: # continuous example with a regular ggplot2 p <- ggplot(mtcars, aes(x = wt, y = mpg, color = mpg)) +       geom_point(size = 3) +       labs(title = "mtcars: mpg", subtitle = "Default scale")  # you can specify which column to pull values from p_scaled <- plot_color_scale(plot = p, val_col = "mpg") +               labs(title = "mtcars: mpg", subtitle = "Custom scale")  (p + p_scaled) & athanor::theme_bw_custom & athanor::labels_standard # typical usage with a Seurat DotPlot p <- DotPlot(obj, features = stringr::str_c("Gene", 1:10), assay = "RNA")  # you can pass a plot in with a pipe p %>% plot_color_scale() # default parameters # can change the colors as desired plot_color_scale(plot = p, palette = rev(pals::brewer.brbg(n = 5)))

title: Main processing pipeline: GEX, concatenation, and WNN
code: library(athanor) library(dplyr) library(ggplot2) library(Matrix) library(patchwork) library(recipes) library(Seurat)  set.seed(42)

what: Simulate and build the initial object
title: Main processing pipeline: GEX, concatenation, and WNN
text: sim_gex_manual() generates sparse count matrix wraps Seurat object. also adds cell_id column metadata, required downstream run_wnn(). also attach small ADT assay (real experiments hold cell surface protein counts) since presence expected run_wnn().
code: num_cells <- 2000 num_genes <- 500 num_proteins <- 6 data_desc <- "Simulated"  # TODO: put the memory and naive B cells into one cluster obj <- sim_gex_splatter(num_genes = num_genes, num_cells = num_cells,                         splatter_groups = c(0.4, 0.5, 0.1),                         splatter_method = "groups")  # add a small ADT assay adt_counts <- Matrix(as.integer(rexp(num_proteins * num_cells, rate = 0.3)),                      nrow = num_proteins, ncol = num_cells, sparse = TRUE) rownames(adt_counts) <- c("CD19", "CD21", "CD27", "CD38", "IGD", "IGM") colnames(adt_counts) <- Cells(obj) obj[["ADT"]] <- CreateAssayObject(counts = adt_counts)  # confirm that cell_id is present head(obj$cell_id) #>   Cell1   Cell2   Cell3   Cell4   Cell5   Cell6  #> "Cell1" "Cell2" "Cell3" "Cell4" "Cell5" "Cell6"

title: Main processing pipeline: GEX, concatenation, and WNN
text: seurat_pipeline() runs normalization, variable feature selection, scaling, PCA, neighbor finding, UMAP. Passing nfeatures_RNA = 0 perc_mt = 100 skips QC filtering (appropriate simulated data mitochondrial genes). higher cluster_res gives granular clusters, useful annotation.
code: obj <- seurat_pipeline(obj, nfeatures_RNA = 0, perc_mt = 100,                        num_features = num_genes, num_pcs = 15, num_dims = 10,                        k_param = 20, cluster_res = 0.5, verbose = FALSE)  # reductions and cluster column produced names(obj@reductions) #> [1] "rpca"     "rna.umap" levels(obj$seurat_clusters) #> [1] "0" "1" "2" plot_dimplot(obj, title = "GEX", data_source = data_desc,              meta_col = "seurat_clusters", reduc = "rna.umap")

title: Main processing pipeline: GEX, concatenation, and WNN
text: add_annotations() maps data frame cluster--cell-type assignments onto Seurat object. data frame must one row per cluster order levels(seurat_clusters).
code: # TODO: annotate on a cell level instead to simulate automated annotation  n_clusters <- nlevels(obj$seurat_clusters)  # one cell type label per cluster # cell_types <- sample(c("Memory B cells", "Naive B cells", "Plasma cells", #                        "Transitional B cells"), size = n_clusters, #                      replace = TRUE, prob = c(0.4, 0.4, 0.1, 0.1))  # we chose to have 3 clusters during simulation, so let's define them directly cell_types <- c("Memory B cells", "Naive B cells", "Plasma cells")  annotations_df <- data.frame(CellType = cell_types)  obj <- add_annotations(seurat_obj = obj, annotations_df = annotations_df,                        cell_types_col = "CellType",                        clusters_col = "seurat_clusters",                        annotations_col = "annotated_clusters")  table(obj$annotated_clusters) #>  #> Memory B cells  Naive B cells   Plasma cells  #>           1023            762            215 plot_dimplot(obj, data_source = data_desc,              clrs_specific = named_colors$cell_types_celltypist,              title = "GEX", reduc = "rna.umap",              meta_col = "annotated_clusters",              plot_label = FALSE, legend_label = "Cell Type")

title: Main processing pipeline: GEX, concatenation, and WNN
text: real experiment columns come gex_add_airr() applied AIRR-formatted table. simulate directly downstream steps self-contained. Can also numeric isotype = BCR isotype (categorical) mu_freq = somatic hypermutation frequency (numeric)
code: obj$cdr3_aa_length <- factor(sample(c("Short", "Medium", "Long"), num_cells,                                     replace = TRUE),                              levels = c("Short", "Medium", "Long"),                              ordered = TRUE) obj$isotype <- factor(sample(c("IgM", "IgD", "IgG", "IgA"), num_cells,                              replace = TRUE,                              prob = c(0.35, 0.15, 0.35, 0.15))) obj$mu_freq <- round(runif(num_cells, min = 0, max = 0.3), 3)  obj[[]] %>%   select(mu_freq, cdr3_aa_length, isotype) %>%   summary() #>     mu_freq       cdr3_aa_length isotype   #>  Min.   :0.0000   Short :675     IgA:305   #>  1st Qu.:0.0770   Medium:667     IgD:264   #>  Median :0.1550   Long  :658     IgG:709   #>  Mean   :0.1514                  IgM:722   #>  3rd Qu.:0.2260                            #>  Max.   :0.3000

title: Main processing pipeline: GEX, concatenation, and WNN
text: concatenate_gex_bcr() combines RNA counts processed BCR feature columns new RNA_BCR assay, runs PCA UMAP joint space. pca_stage = "" means BCR features concatenated PCA; set "" combine pre-computed GEX BCR PCA embeddings instead. cols_to_include lists BCR metadata columns pull assay. Internally, process_bcr_features() normalizes numeric columns, converts ordered factors ordinal scores, one-hot encodes categoricals.
code: # keep a copy so run_wnn() below starts from the same object obj_cat <- concatenate_gex_bcr(obj, pca_stage = "Before",                                cols_to_include =                                  c("cdr3_aa_length", "isotype", "mu_freq"),                                normalize = TRUE, num_dims = 10)  # new assay and reductions names(obj_cat@assays) #> [1] "RNA"     "ADT"     "RNA_BCR" names(obj_cat@reductions) #> [1] "rpca"         "rna.umap"     "rna_bcr.pca"  "rna_bcr.umap" plot_dimplot(obj_cat, data_source = data_desc,              clrs_specific = named_colors$cell_types_celltypist,              title = "Concatenated GEX & BCR", reduc = "rna_bcr.umap",              meta_col = "annotated_clusters",              plot_label = FALSE, legend_label = "Cell Type")

title: Main processing pipeline: GEX, concatenation, and WNN
text: run_wnn() integrates two modalities via Weighted Nearest Neighbors. expects: Seurat object cell_id column metadata BCR embeddings matrix (features × cells) whose column names match cell_id simulate immune2vec-style embedding matrix directly.
code: num_dims_bcr <- 20  bcr_embeddings <-   matrix(round(runif(num_cells * num_dims_bcr, min = -0.6, max = 0.6), 9),          nrow = num_dims_bcr, ncol = num_cells) rownames(bcr_embeddings) <- paste0("Dim-", seq_len(num_dims_bcr)) colnames(bcr_embeddings) <- obj$cell_id  # must match cell_id  # verify alignment all(colnames(bcr_embeddings) == obj$cell_id) #> [1] TRUE obj_wnn <- run_wnn(obj, embeddings = bcr_embeddings,                    embedding_type = "Simulated",                    pc_gex = 10, pc_bcr = 10, k_param = 20, cluster = TRUE,                    cluster_res = list("GEX" = 0.5, "BCR" = 0.5, "WNN" = 0.5),                    verbose = FALSE)  # bin the mutation frequency for plotting obj_wnn <- bin_mu_freq(obj_wnn)  names(obj_wnn@reductions) #> [1] "rpca"     "rna.umap" "bpca"     "bcr.umap" "wnn.umap"
